canonical_definition: The ability of a model to maintain performance on input sequences that are longer than those it was exposed to during its training phase.

relationships.paper: Less Mature is More Adaptable for Sentence-level Language Modeling
relationships.evidence: We use the LV-Eval benchmark, as proposed by (Yuan et al., 2024a), which subjects LLMs to a more challenging evaluation by inserting confusing facts into the context and by replacing keywords and phrases in the context to make sure that LLMs use comprehension of the context, rather than prior knowledge, to answer the questions. (Section 4.2)

acl25: Less Mature is More Adaptable for Sentence-level Language Modeling

relationships.paper: HyKGE: A Hypothesis Knowledge Graph EnhancedRAGFramework for Accurate and Reliable MedicalLLMs Responses
relationships.evidence: RULER (Hsieh et al., 2024) offers flexible sequence lengths and task complexities with 13 sub-task categories, including retrieval and question answering. We supplement more details about these benchmark in Appendix E. (Section 5.2)

acl25: HyKGE: A Hypothesis Knowledge Graph EnhancedRAGFramework for Accurate and Reliable MedicalLLMs Responses

relationships.paper: HyKGE: A Hypothesis Knowledge Graph EnhancedRAGFramework for Accurate and Reliable MedicalLLMs Responses
relationships.evidence: In addition, our method remains competitive on Llama3-8B and Qwen2 while outperforming others on Mistral on LongBench (Bai et al., 2023), which is the first bilingual benchmark for long context understanding. (Section 6.1)
